title: GitHub Actions Workflow Basics
content:
### GitHub Actions Workflow YAML

A GitHub Actions workflow is a YAML file placed in `.github/workflows/` in your repository. It defines an automated process triggered by events.

### Workflow Structure

```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: echo "Hello, world!"
```

### Key Fields

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **name** | The name of the workflow displayed in the Actions tab |
| **on** | The event that triggers the workflow (push, pull_request, etc.) |
| **jobs** | A collection of jobs to run (parallel by default) |
| **runs-on** | The runner environment (ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest) |
| **steps** | Sequential tasks within a job |
| **uses** | References a reusable action (e.g., `actions/checkout@v4`) |
| **run** | Runs a shell command directly |

### The `actions/checkout` Action

The most commonly used action is `actions/checkout@v4`. It checks out your repository so that the workflow can access its contents. Almost every workflow starts with this step.

### Simple Build Workflow

```yaml
name: Build and Test

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Build
        run: npm run build
      - name: Test
        run: npm test
```

Workflows live in `.github/workflows/` and each file defines one workflow. You can have multiple workflow files for different purposes (CI, CD, linting, etc.).
codeExample:
name: Node.js CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  ci:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build --if-present
      - run: npm test

quiz.question: What does the `uses: actions/checkout@v4` step do?
Where are GitHub Actions workflow files stored?
